detaileddescription.para: Test if there is a child-value with the given index, name or name-path.

simplesect.para: true if there is a value.

detaileddescription.para: Get the child-value at the specified index, name or name-path.

simplesect.para: The child value or a nullptr if the index is out of range.

detaileddescription.para: Convert this value to a given type.
Only converts this type or returns its default value. For example, toText() does not convert an Integer value into text. Use the toTextRepresentation() function for such conversions.

simplesect.para: The value of the requested type, or if this value has a different type, a default value.

detaileddescription.para: Get a value and throw if it is not found.
These functions work like value(), but instead of returning a nullptr when the value is not found they throw an Error exception.

parameterdescription.para: (NotFound) if there is no value at the given index.

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, Integer defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> Integer
memberdef.name: getInteger

detaileddescription.para: Get a value of a given type at the given name path.

Try to get a value at the given value path with a given type. If there is no value at the name-path or the value does not have the expected type - a default value passed as defaultValue parameter is returned.

parameterdescription.para: The name path to resolve, relative to this value.
The default value returned if the value can't be resolved.

simplesect.para: The value or defaultValue if there is no matching value at namePath.

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, bool defaultValue=false) const noexcept -> bool
memberdef.name: getBoolean

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, Float defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> Float
memberdef.name: getFloat

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, const String &defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> String
memberdef.name: getText

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, const Date &defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> Date
memberdef.name: getDate

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, const Time &defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> Time
memberdef.name: getTime

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, const DateTime &defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> DateTime
memberdef.name: getDateTime

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, const Bytes &defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> Bytes
memberdef.name: getBytes

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, const TimeDelta &defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> TimeDelta
memberdef.name: getTimeDelta

memberdef.argsstring: (const NamePath &namePath, const String &defaultValue={}) const noexcept -> String
memberdef.name: getRegEx

detaileddescription.para: Get a value of a given type at the given name path.
These functions throw an Error if no value exists at namePath or if the value does not have the expected type.

parameterdescription.para: The name path to resolve, relative to this value.

detaileddescription.para: Test if a value is of a certain type.

simplesect.para: true if the value has the tested type.

detaileddescription.para: The name.
The name path.
Test if this value has a parent.
The parent.
The type of this value.
Test if this value has location info.
Get the location info for this value.
Set the location info for this value.
Get the number of children.
Get an iterator to the first child value.
Get an iterator to the end of the child values.
Convert this value to the given type.
This is a convenience method, to convert a value to one of the supported types. It is implemented calling the various to<Type> methods.

@important
There are overloads for all integers, converting the signed 64-bit integer into the desired type with the risk of losing information.
There are overloads for all float types, converting a double into a float if necessary with the risk of losing information.
In this implementation, it is not possible to convert regular expression types, as they use String which only convert Text values using toText().

parameterdescription.para: The type to convert this value to.

simplesect.para: The value or a default value if it cannot be converted into the requested type.

detaileddescription.para: Convert this value to its text representation.
Converts the types: Text, Integer, Float, Boolean, Date, Time, Date-Time, Bytes, TimeDelta, RegEx. Sections and lists result in an empty string.

simplesect.para: A string with the text or text representation.

detaileddescription.para: Convert this value to its test adapter representation.
This is used by the test adapter to verify the value, as described in the language documentation.

The general format is <Type>(<value>), where <Type> is one of the standardized type names and <value> the value representation as specified. For example, an integer value 5, is converted into the text Integer(5). No additional info is added to sections.
Test if this container is empty.
Get the first value of a container.
Get the last value of a container.
The base class and interface for all values.
@tested ValueTest
